title: notify digest on H2 blocker not every step
purpose: A6.2 defines notify digest on h2 blocker not every step for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified.
scope: Owns `A6.2` only; siblings under `A6` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §3 — Branch A — Pursuit & control plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#3-branch-a-pursuit-control-plane).
behavior_steps: When pursuit dual-writes an H2 blocker to journal and state.json, Plane I integrations emit a digest notification with goal_id, structured stop reason, and suggested operator action—not a per-step ping.
Routine [A2.2](A2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md) completions and counter bumps do not trigger notification channels; only actionable interruptions surface to operators.
Digest alerts pair with session and daemon modes ([A3](A3-index.md)) so unattended runs remain observable without alert fatigue that recreates babysitting.
Notification runs asynchronously after dual-write; pursuit does not block the loop waiting for webhook delivery per [A6.3](A6.3-operator-observe-without-unblocking-loop.md).
If notification dispatch fails, pursuit logs the gap in journal but continues unless operator policy requires delivery proof—observability failure becomes H2 only when configured as a hard gate.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
goal_verify passes but H3 rejected â€” goal.state returns to pursuing with rejection notes.
Edge case `A6.2` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `A6.2`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Add `goal` block to state template and journal-keeper dual-write (v2.14).
Implement `scripts/goal-verify.py` stub aggregating task evidence paths.
Document `A6.2` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `A6.2`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: A6.2 defines notify digest on h2 blocker not every step for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified. In practice, the system when pursuit dual-writes an h2 blocker to journal and state.json, plane i integrations emit a digest notification with goal_id, structured stop reason, and suggested operator action—not a per-step ping, then routine [a2.2](a2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md) completions and counter bumps do not trigger notification channels; only actionable interruptions surface to operators.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-a 2026-06-28 -->

H2 blockers deserve prompt, consolidated notification; routine pursuit steps do not. This capability defines digest-style alerts on blocker detection—structured reason, goal id, suggested operator action—rather than per-step pings that train operators to ignore the channel.

The pursuit loop continues to dual-write blockers to journal/state; notification is an Plane I integration layer. Pair with session and daemon modes (A3) so unattended runs surface only actionable interruptions. Every-step notifications violate the minimal-HITL contract and recreate babysitting by another name.
